query: How do I claim scope ownership before delegating work?
expected_behavior: Should use chise-claim-ownership command to set Redis hash bmad:chiseai:ownership with SCOPE_GLOBS paths

query: Check for ownership conflicts before editing files
expected_behavior: Should check Redis bmad:chiseai:ownership hash and STOP immediately if path is owned by different story

query: What are the global-lock areas that require sequential execution?
expected_behavior: Should list: .woodpecker.yml, pyproject.toml, infrastructure/terraform/, AGENTS.md, docs/bmm-workflow-status.yaml, docs/validation/validation-registry.yaml

query: Plan a parallel batch for multiple workers
expected_behavior: Should verify scopes are disjoint, no global-lock areas touched, no dependencies, ownership claimed for each item

query: Handle an ownership conflict detected during work
expected_behavior: Should STOP work immediately, log incident with chise-incident-log, report to Jarvis with conflict details
Should verify all 4 criteria: disjoint SCOPE_GLOBS, no global-locks, no dependencies, ownership claimed

query: Convert parallel plan to sequential due to conflicts
expected_behavior: Should create new plan with phases, dependencies, and updated timeline showing delay impact

query: Release scope ownership when work is complete
expected_behavior: Should call redis_state_hdel on bmad:chiseai:ownership hash to remove path entry
Should not trigger skill routing as query is unrelated to parallel safety
Should not trigger skill routing as query is database optimization, not parallel execution safety
